name: Christy Pigott
text: Hey, I'm Christy and I am from Ireland. Well, I feel as though some context is needed, I grew up in rural Ireland (farm country) then moved to Dublin before my family decided to spontaneously move to Madrid where I spent my first two years of high school (and learned Spanish). To finish off, I spent the last two years living in London, while 7 schools seems drastic, how it has shaped me is truly invaluable. Now that my lengthy background story has been covered, I can go back to the basics. I am a first year, Economics and International Business Major (concentration maybe in Finance, might dabble in a data science or philosophy minor). Being the sole place I have ever ventured cross Atlantic, Boston is one of my favourite places. For me, it encapsulates everything I loved about Madrid and London in one place. It has the bustling energy of London, everyone here is always moving and busy going from one place to another, with a European feel which I cannot fully describe, nonetheless I feel completely at peace here. To say my interests lie on a broad spectrum would be an understatement, I'll start with my love for being up high, more specifically whether that be on mountain peaks or skyscrapers, it is somewhere I find utter tranquility. With that, I am a true ski fanatic, my family and I take it very seriously (quite a funny scene actually) and thanks to my roots, I am in fact a horse girl (one of my dreams is live in a mountainous region, owning my own horse). I love listening to music, ranging from Reggaeton (when I miss Spain), to Afro beats (reminds me of London), Techno, EDM (concerts = yes), also love instrumental music after playing piano for last 10 years (enemies to lovers relationship here). I will leave you with one fun fact about myself, I can drive a tractor but don't have my car drivers licence, yet, I am starting lessons shortly though. On the topic of learning new things, I am currently working on my constant stoic facial expression, pls reach out with tips. I know first hand only so much can be said in words, so I'm beyond excited to be apart of this incredible community at TAMID and to meet you all in person.

text: Hi everyone! My name is George Kressler, I am from Rumson, NJ, which is right across from NYC, so about a 30 min ferry boat ride from my house! I am a second year student, and I just transferred to Northeastern from the University of San Diego. I loved my time there, but am happy now, being closer to home. I am studying Computer Science and Business Administration with a concentration in FinTech. I love indoor rock climbing. I got into it heavily in San Diego, and I was thinking about doing the club here but I have class conflicts most nights so if anyone here climbs let me know! I saw Kaya and a few others share some of their bucket list ideas from the meeting and I loved that so here are a few of mine too (only oddly specific ones allowed): Learn to ride a horse, but then ride one in France on the Normandy beaches. Meet Post Malone (my favorite music artist) and give him my converse and take his. Take a photo (I love photography) that gets used in the New York Times, or some other large publication. Later in life, go to paramedic school just to learn. And a couple more little facts about me, I am part of my hometown's volunteer EMS squad (60%+ of firefighters and EMS personnel in the US are volunteers) and am super involved there when I am home. I respond to about 200-300 calls a year and have had a lot of really impactful experiences doing this, and I highly suggest other people see about doing this in their towns because chances are your department needs more members! I love photography, all types but I really take a lot of film pics, so chances are if you're out with me I am bringing my film camera haha. I also love Formula 1, my favorite driver was Daniel Ricardio, but my backup favorite has always been George Russell. And one more thing is I make music! I have done it under a lot of names since I started when I was in 5th grade, but you can find my recent stuff under the name "georgie." on spotify and apple music etc, and I have an EP coming next friday (oct 11)! I am from a family of 6 (including my parents and me), with a huge extended family on my moms side, so family and community is really important to me and I am so excited to join TAMID to find that community at NEU. My brother is a quadriplegic (paralyzed) from a mountain biking accident in 2020, and so I am super involved in local charities, and specifically I do some emergency web development stuff to help families direct community support to areas where they can donate in the immediate hours after an accident. With those projects I've raised over $150,000 for my own family, and helped other families raise over $6,000,000 for their loved ones. Sorry this message is so long! But I am a very visual person so for those like me I attached a few pics to show ya who I am in some ways. Lastly, I am so excited to get involved in TAMID, and meet all of you. Thank you for giving me this opportunity. Please feel free to reach out to me at any time!

To all the New Yorkers, CT has the best pizza in the world (Sally's and Frank Pepe's speak for themselves) so sit back, relax, and enjoy the feeling of second place. Outside the classroom, I'm super into sports. Long-distance running, football, basketball, tennis, spike ball, pickleball, you name it I got it. I vibe with the outdoors, whether it's going on fall nature hikes in upstate CT or playing football in the snow before settling down on my couch, grabbing a nice cup of hot chocolate, and watching the refs bail the Chiefs out for the 15th game of the NFL season. When it comes to music, there are two undeniable truths, 2000s music is the best, and 50 Cent is the best rapper oat. But other than that, I enjoy listening to any kind of music, be it rock or R&B. Recently, I've been trying to get back into shape to run a half-marathon. I want to get some runs in by the Charles River. Over the weekends, I love heading over to Marino to hoop for a bit. I'm happy to play with anyone interested, but just know that like Lavar Ball, I neva lost one on one. I'm super pumped to be part of TAMID. The laughter, the cries, the joy, the sadness, I'm here for each and every one of you, and the sky is the limit for the great things we can do to make this organization the best that it can be!

text: Hi everyone! My name is Angelina Tung, and I am a first year psychology and economics major. I'm super excited to be a part of TAMID! I can't wait to meet everyone and look forward to all the amazing memories we will make!

name: Connor Karr
text: Hey everyone! I'm Connor, and I am excited to be joining TAMID! I'm a second-year student studying Computer Science and Economics. I'm originally from the tiny town of New Canaan, CT. Right now, I've been listening to Mac Miller, Smino, BigXThePlug, Kendrick, beabadoobee, Lil Yachty, and Black Country, New Road. name: Alvin Reji
text: Hey everyone! My name is Alvin Reji, and I'm a second-year studying Mechanical Engineering with a minor in Aerospace engineering. I'm from New Hyde Park, NY which is a town in Long Island. Going from a college surrounded by acres of forests to a city was really a big change and I'm all for it! In my free time I like to be active or social. name: Madhav Nair
text: Hi everyone! My name is Madhav, and I am a second year majoring in computer science with a concentration in AI. I am from San Jose, CA (about an hour south of San Francisco). I spent my first year in Oakland. A lot of people who spent their first year in Oakland always talk about how bad it was, but coming from California, I absolutely loved it. It was such a unique experience that I grew a lot from. However, we are onto bigger and better things here in Boston. I am really big into sports. I have played basketball since I was in elementary school, and I would say it is my favorite sport to play and watch. Football is a close second. Niners and Warriors for life!! However, I wasn't good enough at either sport to play in high school, so I ran track instead. I fell in love with the sport, and I committed a lot of time into it in high school. I honestly find it hard to meet other people who have ran track, so if you have, please hit me up! If you catch me listening to music, I am probably listening to Travis Scott or Drake. I am pretty confident that I can name any Travis Scott song within the first 2 seconds of the song. I am almost there with Drake, but he just has too much music. I have been expanding my taste in the past year. I have started listening to a lot of Tory Lanez, Brent Faiyaz, and Bryson Tiller. I always thought I was a huge music fan until I met some of my friends who listened to music for over 100,000 minutes in a year. I don't know how they could possibly do that. I loved everyone I talked to during recruitment, and I am excited to get to know everyone else. My goal by the end of this semester is to know at least one fun fact about every member in TAMID.

name: Rishi Dilip
text: A little tardy to the introductions but as they say better late than never. My name is Rishi. I am from a suburb 20 minutes out from Seattle. I am currently a first-year majoring in Computer Science and Business. Within TAMID, I am enrolled in TCF and looking to go down the TC path in the future. I am a huge sports lover. Growing up, I couldn't stick to a sport, which meant I was a jack of all trades but, unfortunately, master of none. So I have played cricket, soccer, flag football, basketball, and finally, tennis, for which I played doubles on my high school varsity team. I am also a very passionate fan of many of those sports, most notably football, where I am a diehard Seahawks fan (As many of you can imagine, now living in Boston, I am constantly reminded about the 1-yard line of a certain Superbowl). I am also a big-time car enthusiast, so much so that my father brings me along every time he purchases a new car because I usually know more about the car in question than the salesman. In the summer of junior year, my friend and I took apart his BMW e92 335i. As part of the process, we wrapped the car, installed a new exhaust, replaced the intake, and tuned the car. To this day, it is one of my favorite projects that I worked on. Now, I also love driving cars; I am fortunate enough to have taken some of my dad's cars on the track, as well as a few supercars over the years. I love to travel. Some of my favorite places I have visited are Switzerland, specifically Lucerne and Interlaken, New Zealand (South Island), and Italy. Still on my list is: Africa, Iceland, and Greece. In terms of Music in recent years, my taste has diversified. On the hip-hop side, I am a big Travis Scott fan, and I was lucky to get floor tickets for Utopia. Additionally, I like Drake, Don Toliver, 21, and Ye (only his music). On the R&B front, I love Tory, PND, Bryson Tiller, and Brent Faiyaz. Additionally, there is a bit of old 80s rock in Bryan Adams and Richard Marx. Growing up in the pop era, I have a throwback playlist with old hits from Charlie Puth, Ed Sheeran, Bruno Mars, The Weeknd, One Direction, Maroon 5, etc. My roommate has even tried to convert me down the country path, but needless to say, it is still a work in progress. It's been great to meet many of you over the past few weeks and I can't wait to get to know everyone through my time at TAMID.
